title: aspidium thelypteris var. noveboracense
intro: Aspidium thelypteris var. noveboracense, commonly known as the New York fern, is a variety of the aspidium thelypteris species, which is a type of deciduous fern native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The New York fern is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its herbal preparations can be used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
